model_trust_notes.ensemble: All models combined. Best default pick. Can share the same blind spot.
model_trust_notes.elo_baseline: Standard sports betting baseline based on past wins/losses. Good long-run read. Slow on sudden changes.
model_trust_notes.glm_ridge: Linear pregame model anchored by recent scoring margin, shot volume, free-throw pressure, and rating signals. It is strongest before tournament-context injuries materially change rotations.
model_trust_notes.glm_elastic_net: Elastic-net pregame model using the NCAAM linear feature map with added shrinkage on overlapping scoring-form and rating signals.
model_trust_notes.glm_lasso: Lasso pregame model using the NCAAM linear feature map with stronger pruning on overlapping scoring-form and rating signals.
model_trust_notes.dynamic_rating: Hot/cold meter. Good for momentum. Can overreact to short streaks.
model_trust_notes.rf: Machine learning model that blends many different predictions from random slices of past games. Good at smoothing out flukes. Can be too cautious on close matchups.
model_trust_notes.goals_poisson: Score-based model. Good for normal scoring games. Messy games hurt it.
model_trust_notes.gbdt: Machine learning model that finds hidden combos. Sometimes too confident.
model_trust_notes.two_stage: Machine learning model with two steps: first predicts game type (fast/slow, close/lopsided), then predicts winner. Good when style matchups matter. If step 1 is wrong, final pick can be wrong.
model_trust_notes.bayes_bt_state_space: Tracks team strength after every game and gives a range, not just one number. Good for spotting rising/falling teams with uncertainty shown. Can move fast after injuries, trades, or short weird stretches.
model_trust_notes.bayes_goals: Scoring strength + confidence meter. Good trend read. Can lag sudden lineup changes.
model_trust_notes.simulation_first: Runs the matchup thousands of times using set assumptions (team strength, pace, and scoring). Good for seeing different paths. If those assumptions are off, this number can be off.

ensemble.headline: Default forecast that blends the live model stack into one probability.
ensemble.trust_note: All models combined. Best default pick. Can share the same blind spot.

elo_baseline.headline: Single-rating baseline built from historical results and home/away context.
elo_baseline.trust_note: Standard sports betting baseline based on past wins/losses. Good long-run read. Slow on sudden changes.

glm_ridge.headline: Pregame ridge logistic regression driven by scoring margin, shot profile, and rating context.
glm_ridge.trust_note: Linear pregame model anchored by recent scoring margin, shot volume, free-throw pressure, and rating signals. It is strongest before tournament-context injuries materially change rotations.

glm_elastic_net.headline: Pregame elastic-net logistic regression driven by the current NCAAM feature map.
glm_elastic_net.trust_note: Elastic-net pregame model using the NCAAM linear feature map with added shrinkage on overlapping scoring-form and rating signals.

glm_lasso.headline: Pregame lasso logistic regression driven by the current NCAAM feature map.
glm_lasso.trust_note: Lasso pregame model using the NCAAM linear feature map with stronger pruning on overlapping scoring-form and rating signals.

dynamic_rating.headline: Fast-moving strength estimate that reacts more quickly than Elo.
dynamic_rating.trust_note: Hot/cold meter. Good for momentum. Can overreact to short streaks.

rf.headline: 44 active inputs from the current NCAAM feature map.
rf.trust_note: Machine learning model that blends many different predictions from random slices of past games. Good at smoothing out flukes. Can be too cautious on close matchups.

goals_poisson.headline: Goal-rate model that turns projected scoring into a win probability.
goals_poisson.trust_note: Score-based model. Good for normal scoring games. Messy games hurt it.

gbdt.headline: 40 active inputs from the current NCAAM feature map.
gbdt.trust_note: Machine learning model that finds hidden combos. Sometimes too confident.

two_stage.headline: 30 active inputs from the current NCAAM feature map.
two_stage.trust_note: Machine learning model with two steps: first predicts game type (fast/slow, close/lopsided), then predicts winner. Good when style matchups matter. If step 1 is wrong, final pick can be wrong.

bayes_bt_state_space.headline: Bayesian rating layer that tracks team strength with uncertainty over time.
bayes_bt_state_space.trust_note: Tracks team strength after every game and gives a range, not just one number. Good for spotting rising/falling teams with uncertainty shown. Can move fast after injuries, trades, or short weird stretches.

bayes_goals.headline: Bayesian scoring model that estimates team strength from expected scoring rates.
bayes_goals.trust_note: Scoring strength + confidence meter. Good trend read. Can lag sudden lineup changes.

simulation_first.headline: Scenario simulator that turns repeated matchup draws into a probability estimate.
simulation_first.trust_note: Runs the matchup thousands of times using set assumptions (team strength, pace, and scoring). Good for seeing different paths. If those assumptions are off, this number can be off.
